course_description:
This course integrates technical mining principles, sustainability and modern financial evaluation techniques to generate and value strategic mine plans. It deepens the understanding and use of key levers for value creation as part of the strategic mine planning process so that maximum value is able to be derived from the extraction and processing of a mineral resource for all stakeholders, over the life of the mine and beyond. This course enhances the ability of students to generate, critically analyse and justify a strategic mine plan and any trade-offs that may be required, from first principles as well as using standard industry software.

This course integrates technical mining principles, sustainability and modern financial evaluation techniques to generate and value strategic mine plans. It deepens the understanding and use of key levers for value creation as part of the strategic mine planning process so that maximum value is able to be derived from the extraction and processing of a mineral resource for all stakeholders over the life of the mine and beyond. This course enhances the ability of students to generate, critically analyse and justify a strategic mine plan and any trade-offs that may be required, from first principles as well as using standard industry software.

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
This course has been timed to avoid clashes with all other courses in this year level within the mining engineering discipline. If youﾠare enrolled in aﾠcourse that clashes with this courseﾠit is most likely a result ofﾠdeviating from theﾠprogram structure or taking additional courses from other year levels or courses outside of the mining engineering discipline. As this is a core compulsory course for mining engineers it is recommended that you attend this course and make alternative arrangements with coordinatorsﾠfrom other courses.
course_aims: This course aims to exhibit the principles and practical methodologies of strategic mine planning and mine valuation to maximise the financial, ethical and sustainable value for all stakeholders over the life of the mine and beyond.

learning_outcomes.description: Apply traditional financial valuation methodologies and the concepts of marginal, fixed and total costs directly relating to a resource project.
Determine and justify the optimal mine planning strategy according to the key levers for value creation for stakeholders, given local constraints
Critically reflect on a proposed mining plan using Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Knowledges and Perspectives to determine the sustainability implications for key stakeholders.
Apply modern optimisation algorithms in standard industry software to support the decision-making process for generating the strategic mine plan.
Assess and justify the viability of strategic mining plans using financial technical modelling to determine financial, technical and sustainable trade-offs.
Determine the post-mining environmental impacts to recommend modifications to mining plans for suitable post-mining land use.
Work effectively in teams to communicate on the financial, technical and sustainable outcomes of a mining project.

assessment_details.title: Take home assignment
assessment_details.task_description: This is a take home task to be completed individually. It will comprise of a series of short answer and calculation questions relating to material covered in weeks 1-6. The value of each question will be provided upon release of the item. Further details about this task will be provided closer to its release. Please refer to Blackboard for detailed marking criteria. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Please prepare a written report that provides the working and answer to each question. Submission of excel files is not appropriate. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This is submitted via Turnitin on Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

assessment_details.title: Manual Planning Project
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This project is to be completed individually. It will comprise of a series of tasks required in the strategic planning of a mining operation. The value of each task will be provided upon release of the item. Further details about this task will be provided closer to its release. No specialist mine planning software is to be used in this task. Complete working in Excel only. Please prepare a slide pack that provides the answer to each task. Submission of excel files are also required which shows detailed working within the template file that will be provided. This project will culminate in an individual interview after the submission of the slide pack and excel files at a date to be determined. Please refer to Blackboard for detailed marking criteria. This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Submission via Blackboard by the due date/time.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
As the interview component is timed-assessment, Student Access Plans (SAPs) are not suitable supporting documentation and discretionary requests are not available. Students must provide evidence that demonstrates their inability to attend the assessment at the scheduled time.
Students submitting an extension must indicate whether the extension request is for the written component or the interview component.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
Interview Attendance: 100% late penalty as the nature of the assessment does not allow for the application of a late penalty on a sldiing scale.
